page: Microsoft Azure D2as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core of an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, utilizing the x86_64 architecture with simultaneous multithreading. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% of stress-ng benchmarks, and single-threaded gzip and bzip3 compression tests show average performance. However, multi-core performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng and the bottom 10% for Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with read-only small block cached bandwidth scoring in the bottom 10% and uncached large block read/write speeds landing in the bottom 25%. Database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput also rank in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D2as_v5 offers a low-resource, dedicated x86_64 hardware profile without the overhead of local storage or high-capacity memory arrays. This makes it a cost-effective option for workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation but do not demand high memory throughput or parallel processing power. Suitable workloads include lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, minor background utility tasks, and development or testing environments where multi-core scaling and high memory bandwidth are not critical.
description: Microsoft Azure D2as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv5 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs running at 3.525 GHz, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, alongside average single-threaded compression and text processing speeds. However, it exhibits poor multi-core performance and memory bandwidth, both falling into the bottom 10% to 25% of benchmarks. Lacking a GPU and local storage, this instance is a low-resource option for workloads that prioritize single-threaded execution over parallel processing. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and development or testing environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2as_v5 is a general-purpose VM featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. It offers strong single-core performance but exhibits limited multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2as_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose VM with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU virtual machine featuring strong single-core AMD EPYC performance for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz with 2 dedicated vCPUs.
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and 32 MB of L3 cache.
Strong single-core performance, ranking in the top 25% of stress-ng benchmarks.
No local storage or GPU included, with support for Premium Storage.
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications and development environments.
